codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of three homepage sections (BrandFeature, EditorialCards, PressLogos). All three components follow project conventions: zero border-radius (globally enforced), correct design tokens (text-primary for terracotta, bg-cream/bg-cream-dark for backgrounds), proper font families (font-heading for serif headlines, font-body for body text, label-text utility for eyebrows), 0.7s cubic-bezier transitions, Next.js Image with fill/sizes/data-placeholder, and responsive layouts. TypeScript is correct with no type errors. The implementation fully matches the feature description: BrandFeature has ~55% image left with brand copy right including the specified eyebrow/headline/body/CTA, EditorialCards shows a 4-up grid of blog cards with all required elements linking to /blog/[slug], and PressLogos displays 4 placeholder press names with quotes. All sections are correctly ordered in page.tsx after ProductCarousel.

issues.description: BrandFeature uses max-container without section-padding, applying padding manually to the copy side only. This is architecturally correct for a split-layout section where the image should be edge-to-edge, but it differs from the pattern used in other sections like EditorialCards and PressLogos. Consistent approach is fine as long as future split-layout sections follow the same pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Split-layout sections (like BrandFeature and Hero) use max-container without section-padding, applying padding manually to just the content side. This pattern is undocumented in AGENTS.md but is consistently followed across the two split-layout sections. AGENTS.md only documents 'Section padding: 80px vertical, 64px horizontal on desktop' without specifying the split-layout exception.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/sections/BrandFeature.tsx:6 uses only 'max-container', applies px-6/py-16/md:px-16/lg:px-16 to content div. Same pattern in src/components/sections/Hero.tsx:7 which also uses only 'max-container' with manual padding on the copy side.

summary: Feature passes review. All three homepage sections (BrandFeature, EditorialCards, PressLogos) are implemented correctly per the feature description and design system. Code is clean TypeScript with proper responsive layouts, correct design tokens, and appropriate use of shared data from constants. No blocking issues found. The worker followed the skill procedure, running tsc/lint and verifying visually with agent-browser. One non-blocking observation about the split-layout padding pattern being undocumented.
